SEARCH
Package neqsim.process.optimization.valuechain

Class EconomicParameters

java.lang.Object
neqsim.process.optimization.valuechain.EconomicParameters
All Implemented Interfaces:
Serializable
public class EconomicParameters extends Object implements Serializable
Economic and carbon parameters shared by the value-chain optimization classes.

Collects the prices, costs and financial assumptions needed to turn a converged NeqSim flowsheet into an economic objective: gas and oil sales prices, the cost of the energy consumed by rotating equipment, a carbon price/tax applied to the associated CO2, the carbon intensity of that energy, and the annual discount rate used for net-present-value calculations. All monetary values are expressed in the configured currency (default "NOK").

The class is a plain, serializable parameter holder with a fluent API so it can be built inline: 

 EconomicParameters econ = new EconomicParameters().setGasPrice(3.0).setPowerCost(0.6)
     .setCo2Tax(1200.0).setCo2IntensityTonnePerMWh(0.20).setDiscountRate(0.08);
Version:
1.0
Author:
NeqSim Development Team
See Also:

    Description

  • Constructor Details

    • EconomicParameters

      public EconomicParameters()
      Creates a set of economic parameters with default North-Sea-style values.

  • Method Details

    • setGasPrice

      public EconomicParameters setGasPrice(double value)
      Sets the gas sales price.
      Parameters:
      value - gas price in currency per Sm3 (must be finite and non-negative)
      Returns:
      this instance for chaining

    • setOilPrice

      public EconomicParameters setOilPrice(double value)
      Sets the oil/condensate sales price.
      Parameters:
      value - oil price in currency per Sm3 (must be finite and non-negative)
      Returns:
      this instance for chaining

    • setPowerCost

      public EconomicParameters setPowerCost(double value)
      Sets the cost of consumed energy.
      Parameters:
      value - energy cost in currency per kWh (must be finite and non-negative)
      Returns:
      this instance for chaining

    • setCo2Tax

      public EconomicParameters setCo2Tax(double value)
      Sets the carbon price/tax.
      Parameters:
      value - carbon price in currency per tonne CO2 (must be finite and non-negative)
      Returns:
      this instance for chaining

    • setCo2IntensityTonnePerMWh

      public EconomicParameters setCo2IntensityTonnePerMWh(double value)
      Sets the carbon intensity of the consumed energy.
      Parameters:
      value - carbon intensity in tonnes CO2 per MWh (must be finite and non-negative)
      Returns:
      this instance for chaining

    • setDiscountRate

      public EconomicParameters setDiscountRate(double value)
      Sets the annual discount rate.
      Parameters:
      value - annual discount rate as a fraction (0.08 = 8%); must be greater than -1
      Returns:
      this instance for chaining

    • setCurrency

      public EconomicParameters setCurrency(String value)
      Sets the reporting currency label.
      Parameters:
      value - the currency label (null is replaced with "NOK")
      Returns:
      this instance for chaining

    • getGasPrice

      public double getGasPrice()
      Gets the gas sales price.
      Returns:
      gas price in currency per Sm3

    • getOilPrice

      public double getOilPrice()
      Gets the oil/condensate sales price.
      Returns:
      oil price in currency per Sm3

    • getPowerCost

      public double getPowerCost()
      Gets the cost of consumed energy.
      Returns:
      energy cost in currency per kWh

    • getCo2Tax

      public double getCo2Tax()
      Gets the carbon price/tax.
      Returns:
      carbon price in currency per tonne CO2

    • getCo2IntensityTonnePerMWh

      public double getCo2IntensityTonnePerMWh()
      Gets the carbon intensity of the consumed energy.
      Returns:
      carbon intensity in tonnes CO2 per MWh

    • getDiscountRate

      public double getDiscountRate()
      Gets the annual discount rate.
      Returns:
      annual discount rate as a fraction

    • getCurrency

      public String getCurrency()
      Gets the reporting currency label.
      Returns:
      the currency label

    • discountFactor

      public double discountFactor(double year)
      Computes the discount factor for a given period.

      The factor is 1 / (1 + r)^year, where r is the annual discount rate. Year 0 returns 1.0 (present value of money today).

      Parameters:
      year - the period index in years (0 = present)
      Returns:
      the discount factor in the range (0, 1]
